old_claim.note: Leather Tanning provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No leather-tanning dependency remains because the cited Ohalo II shelter evidence consists of brush huts built from branches, plant stems, and grasses.

source_shape.source_locator: Shelter and bedding description identifies brush-hut walls and floors made from branches, plant stems, and bundled grasses at Ohalo II.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source-backed shelter construction uses gathered plant material and does not establish any hide covering or leather treatment.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Leather could be optional in other windbreak designs, but it is neither necessary nor present in the evidence currently cited for this node.

ontology_before: All simple windbreaks required tanned leather because hide was one item in a broad material list.
ontology_after: The shelter node can follow its plant-built evidence without an optional hide-processing edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: simple_windbreak_shelters no longer directly depends on leather_tanning.
would_reject_if: Direct Ohalo II evidence established a tanned-hide covering as a required part of the shelter.
short_reason: The cited shelter is plant built and does not evidence leather.
